notes: net_iid/excess_iid: NON-OVERLAPPING evaluation — every 10th test date per fold gives independent 10-day periods; these are the statistically defensible headline numbers (the daily-series sharpe is inflated by overlap autocorrelation up to ~sqrt(H))

overlapping-window approximation: consecutive test dates give overlapping 10-day forward windows; the daily series approximates a top-20 book rebalanced daily with 10-day holds, NOT a path-accurate equity curve
per-day conversion is geometric: daily = (1 + r_H)^(1/10) - 1 for both portfolio and benchmark

costs: steady-state turnover = 1/H = 0.100 of the book/day, 2 sides x 30.0 bps -> 6.00 bps/day; flat per-side bps, no size-aware market impact (ml.eval.impact_cost exists for that when position sizes are known)
benchmark = equal-weight mean realized fwd_return of ALL tradable names each test date (internal, label-derived; no external index data)
per-fold models refit on purged walk-forward train windows exactly as in ml.training.pipeline._cv_and_fit
